events.text: Q1 FY26: adjusted operating EPS $2.74, a beat vs consensus $2.54 and +$0.60 YoY; GAAP EPS $4.49. FY26 guidance reaffirmed at $11.00-12.00/share (midpoint $11.50), with base-earnings growth 'above 20% through 2029.' Repurchased ~1.2M shares at ~$285 ($335M) under the $5B authorization.

Hyperscaler capex in 2026 cited as 'nearly 75% higher than last year.' ~5,000 MW of new resources (nuclear uprates, gas, battery) submitted to PJM's interconnection queue. FCF guided at $8.4B for 2026-2027 and $11.5-13B for 2028-2029.

DC broker: the 'big four' hyperscalers get what they want, Oracle is the IG credit swing and neoclouds need backstops — counterparty credit, not just power, gates the contracting; ~30% of the pipeline is genuinely grid-islanded BTM. Read-through: sharpens the 'deals slip / BYOG threatens visibility' bear.

BTM expert (ex-GE Vernova): behind-the-meter is a permanent solution, not a bridge (grid reconnection raises public tariffs; ISOs 'pick their battles') — read-through to the BYOG threat to the visibility of CEG's contracted fleet.
Midstream DC expert puts BTM at ~40% of power deals — reinforces the bear on IPPs/generators ceding share of DC contracting.

JPM models 138 GW of US DC capacity growth through 2030 (up from 122 GW in Nov-2025) within $5.5tn of AI capex; power remains the most critical constraint. Deal table corroborates the Constellation-Meta Clinton contract, 1.1 GW, 20 years starting June 2027. EPRI: DCs could reach 9-17% of US electricity demand by 2030.

cells: Hyperscaler capex ~75% higher; some talks paused
Strong Q1, in line with plan
Idle turbines available; Calvert Cliffs ~2 GW
~5,000 MW into PJM queue; Byron/Braidwood in plan
